RULES_ALEXG.source:
const RULES_ALEXG={
  ruleVersion:'alex_g_sr_v1',
  originalAlexConcepts:[
    'Support is where buyers hold price up; resistance is where sellers push price down.',
    'Support and resistance are collectively "area of interest" (AOI) -- support is your AOI when buying, resistance when selling.',
    'A zone can never be traded against its current role -- no buying resistance, no selling support.',
    'A single zone is bidirectional: reactions from above AND below the same price area both count toward one shared touch tally.',
    'Three confirmed reactions validate a zone; the fourth (or later) return that produces its own confirmed rejection is the first trade opportunity.',
    'Three named strength tiers: weak (<3 touches), valid (3 touches), strong (4+ touches).',
    'More touches are always better, with no stated ceiling on strength.',
    'Zones must be tight, fitted to where reactions actually clustered -- demonstrated visually, no formula given.',
    'Zones exist on H1, H4, Daily, and Weekly timeframes -- exactly these four, explicitly named.',
    'Higher timeframe zones are more respected than lower timeframe zones.',
    'Break and retest: a failed rejection, then a confirmed break, then price returns and retests from the new side, then the zone role flips.',
    'Psychological round-number levels are additional, non-mandatory confluence, demonstrated at both 500-level and 100-level granularity.',
    'A single soft mention that trend direction increases the odds of a trade working -- never stated as a requirement.'
  ],
  hubTestStandardizations:[
    'Zone-cluster grouping tolerance mechanics and its ATR basis (zoneClusterATRMultiplier) -- EXPERIMENTAL, not tuned against outcomes.',
    'Rejection confirmation window and minimum ATR-normalized displacement thresholds.',
    'Wick strength is recorded but never required (requireWick defaults false) -- the source never mentions wicks.',
    'Break-confirmation close count and choppy-zone penetration/lookback thresholds.',
    'Zone aging is OFF by default (unlimited) -- the source favors older, more-touched zones, contradicting a default that would penalize zone age.',
    'Maximum bars allowed between a Type B break and its retest.',
    'Psychological-level pip-scaling via pipSize() and the dual 500/100 interval choice -- the source uses both granularities inconsistently.',
    'Same-timeframe rejection confirmation (a Weekly zone confirms on a Weekly candle, etc.) -- the best- and only-evidenced approach, never stated as an explicit rule.',
    'Structural trend context computed from confirmed swing structure (not an indicator) -- metadata only, never gates, scores, or alters a trade.',
    'The entire stop-loss/take-profit/risk/R:R mechanism -- 100% unaddressed by the source.',
    'Zero session/day/news filtering in v1.0 -- a deliberate design choice, not a source gap; metadata is still recorded for later analysis.',
    'Structural-conflict recording -- record-only, never blocks entry or alters stop/target/size/qualification.',
    '"Repeated Zone Reaction" and "Break & Retest" are Hub research labels for record-keeping, not Alex\'s own terminology.',
    'v4.2: maxLiveEntryDelayPips (live paper trading only) -- maximum allowed distance between a setup\'s historical qualificationClose and the actual live bid/ask fill before the trade is rejected rather than chased; the source never addresses live execution latency at all.',
    'v4.2.1: maxLiveSignalAgeMinutes (live paper trading only, per-timeframe) -- the deterministic "first polling opportunity" rule: a live setup is evaluated for entry exactly once, the first time it is seen, and is marked IGNORED — STALE SIGNAL rather than attempted if its qualification candle is already older than one bar-period of its own timeframe by that first evaluation (e.g. an H1 setup first seen 90 minutes after it qualified is stale). This is a time-based staleness gate, distinct from and in addition to maxLiveEntryDelayPips\' price-based gate -- the source never addresses signal age either.'
  ],
  config:{
    zoneTimeframes:['H1','H4','D','W'],
    htfPriority:{W:4,D:3,H4:2,H1:1},
    zoneClusterATRMultiplier:0.5,
    atrPeriod:14,
    rejectionConfirmWithinBars:1,
    rejectionDisplacementATRMultiplier:0.25,
    requireWick:false,
    minWickRatio:0.0,
    breakConfirmationCloses:1,
    maxPenetrationsBeforeChoppyFlag:3,
    choppyLookbackBars:50,
    maxZoneAgeBars:null,
    maxBarsBetweenBreakAndRetest:50,
    psychLevelIntervalPips500:500,
    psychLevelIntervalPips100:100,
    trendSwingLookback:3,
    trendSwingScanBars:200,
    stopATRBuffer:0.25,
    riskPercent:1.0,
    minRR:2.0,
    maxLiveEntryDelayPips:5, // v4.2 Hub Test Standardization -- max allowed distance between qualificationClose and the live bid/ask fill before the trade is rejected as ENTRY_MOVED_TOO_FAR_FROM_SIGNAL
    maxLiveSignalAgeMinutes:{H1:60,H4:240,D:1440,W:10080} // v4.2.1 Hub Test Standardization -- one bar-period per timeframe; a setup first evaluated later than this after its own qualificationTimestamp is IGNORED — STALE SIGNAL, never opened
  },
  experimentalParams:{
    zoneClusterATRMultiplier:{
      status:'EXPERIMENTAL',
      sensitivityRange:[0.25,0.5,0.75,1.0],
      note:'Hub Test Standardization -- arbitrary starting point pending controlled sensitivity testing, not tuned against historical outcomes.'
    }
  }
};
